# B01: Detailed Recap

## Meeting Overview
**Date:** 2026-02-18  
**Topic:** Careerspan Talent Pipeline Strategy for US-India Cross-Border Recruitment  
**Participants:** Vrijen Attawar (V), Shivam Desai (CorridorX)  
**Duration:** ~60 minutes  

## Chronological Summary

### Opening & Context — NYC Real Estate & Network
- Shivam shares story about attending his friend's wife's art exhibition in a massive building on 50th Street in Manhattan
- A Texas-based business group partnered with a Bangalore entrepreneur (made money in energy in Minnesota) bought a former UBS corporate office building (~50 stories) at pennies on the dollar (~$10M, transaction public on WSJ)
- The friend's wife opened an Indian historical art and high-end furniture gallery in the building
- Shivam connected with one of the owners for ~30 minutes at the event (~100 attendees, high-end crowd)
- V and Shivam agree these networks are interconnected and relevant to US-India cross-border plays
- Shivam notes the space could be used for future events/gatherings, but this is context — not directly Careerspan business

### Strategic Advantages Discussion
- V frames two core objectives: (1) distribute Careerspan to build a talent base by April/May (July deadline), and (2) collect proof points around placement and tech validation
- Shivam frames it as making Careerspan an "alternate pipeline" from existing recruitment efforts — a separate, high-value flow

### Left Lane Capital — VC Angle
- V met with Nico from Left Lane Capital earlier that day
- Pitched Left Lane on: access to top quality young engineering talent, ability to scan pools quickly, ability to cultivate talent, and long-term reduction of 10-15% recruiter surcharges for portfolio founders
- Nico was responsive to the concept of silent vs. explicit criteria scanning — "covert" scanning capability intrigued them
- V also pitched India-based engineering teams via Shivam/CorridorX for scale hiring
- **Immediate plan:** V to create a Left Lane account, run a scan of the VC analyst role against the full Careerspan pool, package top candidates with analysis quality, and send to Nico by morning
- Nico will take results to senior partner; if persuaded, V wants Shivam to join the partner meeting
- Two potential framings for the partner meeting: (a) Shivam as business partner who uses the tech for candidate scanning (assessment angle), or (b) Shivam has India covered, V has US campuses — full talent sourcing play
- V tempered expectations: "You probably won't find your candidate in our pool, but you'll see the caliber of analysis"

### Social Distribution Test — Left Lane Role
- Shivam offers to promote the Left Lane role across his social channels (hundreds of young Indians in the US)
- V proposes launching it in stealth mode to protect Left Lane's brand while testing Careerspan's pull
- Debate on stealth vs. named brand: Shivam argues people love brands/names and convert better with logos; V argues stealth creates mystique and lets them define appealing archetypes
- V proposes stealth jobs with some logos (from friendly companies/VCs) as a compromise
- V's deeper concept: create synthetic high-caliber job archetypes from cluster analysis of Left Lane portfolio companies, then use those to attract candidates who are tested via Careerspan — effectively a talent pool play

### Talent Pool / AI Talent Agency Concept
- V proposes framing: "CorridorX and Careerspan partnering to create the world's first AI talent agency"
- Anyone scoring above a threshold on Careerspan would be automatically considered for roles
- Show visual examples of how candidates are sold via the decoded resume
- Shivam pushes back: "What are we trying to prove with Left Lane?" — wants to focus on sending good quality volume first

### Validation Gap
- Shivam identifies the core problem: no one has explicitly told them the Careerspan tech/process has been valuable in evaluating candidates
- Docsum only said one resume (sourced by Shivam's vendors) was good — that validated Shivam's sourcing, not Careerspan's tech
- Vikram (previous employer contact) also hasn't given explicit validation
- Both agree Left Lane is the lowest hanging fruit to validate the tech

### Cobalt ID — Priya
- V pinged Priya from Cobalt ID; they've been struggling with hiring
- Half their team is Indian, founder is Indian
- Priya said: "At this point, we'll take anyone" — domestic or India
- V asks Shivam to look into Cobalt for engineering candidates
- Potential team deal: multiple engineering positions, company cultural profile for level-setting
- Shivam raises two models: (a) direct full-time hire managed by the US company, or (b) contract model where an India-based staffing company employs and manages the team, providing replacement guarantees if attrition happens
- V plans to set up a 3-way call (V, Shivam, Priya)

### Sponsor Pipeline — Brex, Manifest, Others
- V proposes getting vendors (Brex, Manifest, etc.) to fund Careerspan distribution
- Pitch to sponsors: own a talent pipeline as a perk for your founders/customers
- Sponsors would pay for Careerspan to spread to top communities/campuses
- In return, sponsors get exclusive access to recruiter-grade talent insight with no placement fee (initially)
- Cost to run: ~$2-3K/month for keeping ~5,000 people engaged, could optimize down 2-5x
- V: "Go to top talent pools and say it's free. Go to Brex and say we'll guarantee inroads into those pools."
- Shivam notes his Brex relationship is currently US-India focused; the talent pipeline pitch is a different angle — he'll figure out how to approach it
- Shivam also pitching Brex on India tours for startups with Delaware C corps

### Shortlist / Morpheus Group — Andrew/Ivar
- V meeting Ivar next week about using Careerspan tech for Shortlist (Andrew's business)
- Shivam: "Careerspan should be branded there on Shortlist"
- V notes Andrew is practical, not trying to build internal tech — good for partnership
- They won't only hire Americans, so there's still placement opportunity for India talent
- Could be another micro-community: Andrew has both employers and operational capability

### Campus Distribution Strategy
- V proposes going to every Indian Student Association (ISA), Harvard South Asian (HSA), etc. across the country
- Dump Careerspan in for free, funded by sponsors like Manifest
- Sponsors get lifetime pipeline, not just resumes — data shows people keep coming back to Careerspan
- V references Emory: they were going to launch Careerspan to their "Wise Hearts" program ($1K+/year donors for 5+ years)

### O-1 Visa / Manifest Deep Dive
- Shivam provides context on O-1 caliber: flying electric taxi builder from IIT Madras, former Google Applied AI lead at Maps (now raised $5M), Schwarzman Scholar building aged care devices
- O-1 ≠ regular tech workers; it's extraordinary ability ("Einstein visa")
- V acknowledges the high bar but argues even F-1 students produce founder-grade people (cites 3 foreign founders from NYU all on F-1s, including one from his own PR program who became a YC-grade founder)
- V proposes Manifest could fund campus access as a marketing play — even if students aren't O-1 targets, the halo effect of being associated with career development for foreigners is valuable
- Shivam wants equity in US-India firms he helps build, not just referral fees
- Discussion on Manifest's business model (success-fee based) and whether volume throughput makes economic sense for them

### Division of Labor — Next Steps
- V: Follow up with Left Lane (send analysis to Nico by morning), follow up with Priya (Cobalt ID), work VC angles, follow up with Emory contact
- Shivam: Talk to Brex tomorrow, continue working Manifest and SVB angles, kickoff in late March
- Both: Sync tomorrow afternoon, meet in person before March 1

### Personal — V & Amanda Separating
- V shares that he and Amanda (wife of ~8 years, married ~3 years) are likely separating
- Living apart for ~4 months
- Core incompatibility: Amanda wants equal parenting and stability; V is a founder who needs to run
- On great terms, planning a "classic millennial divorce" with shared cat custody
- Shivam empathizes, shares his own relationship struggles (broken up 5 times with current girlfriend)
- Shivam offers support: "Let me know how I can help"
- Shivam advises stepping back from the pace occasionally — "Five years from now, no one's going to remember Brex. You're going to remember what happened with Amanda."

### Closing
- Both agree the next month looks exciting
- Plan to catch up in person before March 1
- Will sync tomorrow regardless
